ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301589 'carbonated beverage' is the closest defensible parent class; no exact FoodOn term exists for generic flavoured soda across all flavours. Used closest_parent match type as the term represents a broader category encompassing flavoured carbonated soft drinks.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for generic flavoured carbonated soda; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: citric acid or phosphoric acid (varies by flavour)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve can of flavoured soda is approximately 330 ml (330 g), providing about 139 kcal and 37.6 g of sugar per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured Carbonated Drink — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D06020014 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. Values are accepted as-is from the approved primary source.

gi_source.title: Soft drink, regular cola — Glycemic Index Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No exact GI value exists in the University of Sydney GI Database for a generic multi-flavour soda. GI of 63 is taken from the closest analogous approved-source entry: regular carbonated soft drink (cola-type), University of Sydney GI Database. Flavoured sodas with similar sugar content (10–12 g/100 g sucrose/HFCS) are expected to have a comparable GI in the 58–65 range. GL per serving calculated as: (63 × 10.52 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 330 g serving) / 100 = 22.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage (11.37 g sugar/100 g) — SSB consumption independently raises blood pressure via insulin-mediated sympathetic activation and renal sodium retention, regardless of the low direct sodium content (7 mg/100 g).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid flavoured sodas entirely; replace with plain water, unsweetened sparkling water, or unsweetened herbal tea, as regular SSB intake is independently associated with elevated systolic blood pressure (AHA 2018).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free-sugar content (11.37 g/100 g) in liquid form with zero dietary fibre — liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly, producing acute postprandial glucose spikes and contributing to insulin resistance with habitual consumption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid flavoured sodas completely; even one 330 ml can delivers approximately 37.5 g of free sugar with a glycemic load of ~22, making it one of the highest-risk single-serve beverage choices for blood glucose management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with elevated triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, increased visceral adiposity, and higher risk of coronary heart disease events independent of total caloric intake.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should eliminate flavoured sodas from their diet; habitual SSB intake (≥1 serving/day) is associated with a 20% higher risk of cardiovascular mortality (Malik et al., Circulation 2010).

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar content (11.37 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokines including TNF-alpha and IL-6, with no anti-inflammatory nutrients present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid flavoured sodas; regular consumption is associated with increased disease activity and flare frequency, and should be replaced with unsweetened sparkling water or plain herbal tea.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured soda (any flavour) is a generic category of ultra-processed, sugar-sweetened carbonated soft drinks produced by dissolving sugar and artificial or natural flavourings in carbonated water, typically with added food colouring and acidulants such as citric acid or phosphoric acid. The beverage is clear to lightly coloured, effervescent, and sweet with a flavour profile that varies by variant (e.g., orange, grape, strawberry, lemon-lime). Per 100 g, it provides 42 kcal, 10.52 g carbohydrate, 11.37 g sugar, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g dietary fibre, and 7 mg sodium, as reported by the Singapore HPB Foodo database. The sugar content exceeds the WHO free-sugar daily limit in a single 330 ml can (~37.5 g sugar). Clinically, flavoured sodas are classified as sugar-sweetened beverages (SSBs) and are contraindicated for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. Rapid liquid-sugar absorption produces acute postprandial glucose spikes, and habitual consumption is linked to insulin resistance, elevated triglycerides, reduced HDL, and increased systolic blood pressure. The glycemic index is approximately 63 (medium), yielding a glycemic load of ~22 per standard 330 ml can. This food record covers all flavour variants as a generic category and is not specific to any single brand or flavour.
common_user_queries: Is flavoured soda bad for diabetes?
How much sugar is in a can of flavoured soda?
Can I drink soda if I have high blood pressure?
Is fizzy drink okay for someone with heart disease?
What is the glycemic index of soft drinks?
How many calories in a can of flavoured soda?
Is carbonated flavoured drink suitable for weight loss?
What are the health risks of drinking soda regularly?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id D06020014, review_status reviewed, confidence 0.95). This is an approved primary source for Singapore food records. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using the closest approved-source equivalent (regular carbonated soft drink).
notes: protein_g is naturally zero: flavoured soda contains no protein by formulation — confirmed per HPB Foodo (cr_id D06020014). fat_g is naturally zero: flavoured soda contains no fat by formulation — confirmed per HPB Foodo. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero: no fibre-containing ingredients present. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero by formulation. calcium_mg and iron_mg are confirmed zero per HPB source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for generic flavoured soda not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for cr_id D06020014. GI of 63 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (regular carbonated soft drink); no exact GI entry exists for a generic multi-flavour soda category. GL per serving = (63 × 10.52 / 100) × 330 = 22.1. This record covers all flavour variants generically; individual branded products may vary in sugar content and GI. Ontology: foodon_match_type set to 'closest_parent' as FOODON_03301589 'carbonated beverage' is the nearest parent class in the FoodOn hierarchy; no exact FoodOn term exists for generic flavoured soda.
